text: James made a claim that startled his contemporaries: the education of attention is the education of the whole person. He believed that a person who could voluntarily return their attention to a difficult subject, again and again, possessed the root of judgment, character, and willpower. This was not metaphor but mechanism. Voluntary attention, for James, was not a fixed capacity but a trainable faculty — one strengthened through regular exercise just as a muscle grows through resistance. The modern science of neuroplasticity would later confirm his intuition: deliberate attentional practice physically reshapes the brain circuits involved in self-regulation.
question: What did James believe was the result of training voluntary attention?

options.text: Improved short-term memory and reading speed
The root of judgment, character, and willpower
